Samsø Island

source_file
Bioregional Observatory/4 Bioregional Organizations/Samsø Island.md
type
organization
location_lat
55.9
Place
Samsø, Central Denmark Region, Denmark
place
Samsø Island, Denmark
description
Samsø is a Danish island community internationally recognized for its pioneering transition to 100% renewable energy. The community achieved energy independence through a combination of wind turbines, solar, and biomass district heating systems powered by local agricultural waste, with many installations community-owned. The island has set an ambitious target to be completely fossil fuel free by 2030, initiating conversion to electric transportation starting with the marinas.
id
samsoe-island
website
https://www.visitsamsoe.dk/en/
bioregional_context
Samsø is a small island in the Kattegat Sea between Denmark's Jutland peninsula and Zealand, with a population of around 3,600 residents. Traditionally reliant on agriculture and fishing, the island faced economic decline before reinventing itself through a community-led energy transition.
category
Bioregional Organizer

Samsø Island
